flat_text: Okay. Oh, I can do that. Hello. Hello. Can you hear me? Hello. Just before we go any further, can I confirm your name and age, please? My name is Faiza, and I'm 26. 26, okay. Great. Well, I can give you a patient information leaflet about that, which gives a bit more information about the disease. But it's something that we can readily treat with a couple of options. We have steroid creams I can give you, which will help with the inflammation, and something called emollients, which can help with the dry skin. And that's something you just need to kind of paste onto your skin throughout the day for the next few days, and hopefully that helps your symptoms. Okay. Cool. Thank you very much. You can also try antihistamines. So you can get things over the counter, pyridine, loratadine. That can sometimes help with the itchiness. Yeah, that sounds good. Okay. And that's probably what I'd recommend. Paracetamol on this occasion wouldn't be helpful. Painkillers wouldn't be helpful on this occasion. But let's see how the next few days go. And if it's still not getting better, come back and see me next week, and we can have another look again at your symptoms. Okay. Perfect. Thank you very much. Great. Do you have any questions for me? No. Okay. Well, I wish you all the best. Have a good day. Oh, thank you very much. Bye. Thank you. Bye-bye.
